{{Quote|Mace Windu continues to battle the Separatist droids singlehandedly on Dantooine. Facing an overpowering enemy assault, the unarmed Jedi Master must rely on the Force as he seeks to thwart the seismic tank.|StarWars.com summary|<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/13.html|text=''Clone Wars'': Chapter Thirteen (small)}}</ref>}}
"'''Chapter 13'''" is the thirteenth episode of the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' animated television series, the third episode in [[Clone Wars: Season Two|Season Two]], and the thirteenth episode in [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|Volume One]]. Directed by [[Genndy Tartakovsky]], the episode originally aired on [[Cartoon Network]] on [[March 30]], [[2004]].

==Plot summary==
Surrounded by [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]] and without a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] defends himself against the droids the only way he can—with his bare fists. Augmented by [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], he destroys many droids this way. Then, after the [[Seismic tank/Legends|seismic tank]] fires again, he catches sight of his lightsaber and manages to [[Telekinesis/Legends|retrieve]] it. Armed once more, he makes quick work of both the tank and the remaining droids. The local situation under control, Windu accepts a drink of [[Water/Legends|water]] from the onlooking boy, [[Paxi Sylo]], then heads back into the [[Battle of Dantooine (Clone Wars)|battle]] at large.

==Release and reception==
This episode is arguably the most controversial episode of the series among fans, as some believe that it exaggerates Mace Windu's abilities with the Force. However, [[Leland Chee]] has defended the portrayal in the episode, citing uses of the Force in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="swcomforum">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1230#16292515|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2007-02-12|time=9:34 AM|quote=TPM sets a precedent when we see Obi-Wan and Qui-Gon elude the destroyer droids. Come to think of it, what about Luke's jump from the bottom of the carbon-freezing chamber in ESB?}}</ref>

[[Genndy Tartakovsky]] revealed in his [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] audio commentary that this is his favorite episode of the series, and that he was nervous about presenting the episode to [[George Lucas]] due to the issue mentioned above.
